title: Three-Constraint Elimination for Hodge Surjectivity

analysis.object: T737: Three-Constraint Elimination for Hodge Surjectivity depth analysis

strategy.description: T110 closed RH: ET<3 impossible, ET>3 impossible, ET=inf impossible -> ET=3

hodge_analog_setup.description: Hodge analog: assume non-surjective class h exists. Classify h by depth.
hodge_analog_setup.reason: Three cases: h is depth-shiftable, depth-blocked, or self-contradictory

case_1_depth_shiftable.description: Case 1: h can be made algebraic by deformation -- contradicts being Hodge but not algebraic

case_2_depth_blocked.description: Case 2: h is depth-blocked at EML-inf -- lives at EML-inf permanently
case_2_depth_blocked.reason: But Hodge classes are EML-3, not EML-inf -- contradiction?

case_3_borderline.description: Case 3: h is in the EML-3 to EML-inf transition zone
case_3_borderline.reason: EML-4 would be needed -- but EML-4 does not exist (T564)

elimination_attempt.description: All three cases lead to contradiction IF EML-4 gap is strict
elimination_attempt.reason: The T564 no-EML-4 theorem is the key tool

t737_result.description: Case 3 elimination via no-EML-4: if h is not EML-3 and not EML-inf, it cannot exist
t737_result.reason: T737: three-constraint partially works; Case 2 (h truly EML-inf) remains open

analysis.theorem: T737: Three-Constraint Elimination for Hodge Surjectivity (S1016).

key_theorem: T737: Three-Constraint Elimination for Hodge Surjectivity (S1016).
